Politics.headline: Trump says Iran ceasefire is 'on life support' as hopes for a deal fade
Politics.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that the ceasefire with Iran is faltering due to disagreements on key issues, including demands for war damage compensation and the lifting of the U.S. naval blockade. Iran's parliament speaker has asserted that its armed forces are prepared to respond to any act of aggression.

Politics.headline: Key Republican faces Democratic calls to resign over radio interview
Politics.detail: Rep. Jen Kiggans (R-Va.) is facing calls for resignation from Democratic colleagues after allegedly agreeing with a radio host's remark deemed racially insensitive towards House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries. Kiggans denies the accusations amidst a hotly contested reelection battle.

Politics.headline: Britain's political center crumbles as Reform surges
Politics.detail: The recent local government elections in Britain show a significant rise of the extreme right Reform U.K. party, which gained hundreds of council seats, while Labour and Conservative parties faced setbacks. The Green Party also made gains, and the Liberal Democrats held their ground.

Politics.headline: Alberta's chief electoral officer wants pause on sharing electors list until law amended
Politics.detail: Alberta's chief electoral officer is requesting a temporary halt to sharing the province's list of electors with political parties until legislative changes are implemented to enhance the protection of Albertans' personal information.

Politics.headline: Peter Majar sworn in as Hungary's new prime minister
Politics.detail: Peter Majar was sworn in as Hungary's new prime minister ending 16 years of Victor Orban's authoritarian rule. Majar's pro-EU center-right party won 141 of 199 parliamentary seats last month in a landslide victory.

Finance & Economy.headline: Shipping industry fears fuel shortages as Iran war squeezes bunker fuel supply
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing war with Iran is causing concerns about fuel shortages in the shipping industry, as bunker fuel supplies are being squeezed.

Finance & Economy.headline: U.S. consumer prices rise 3.8% as Iran war sends energy prices higher
Finance & Economy.detail: Consumer prices in the U.S. have increased by 3.8%, driven by rising energy costs due to the ongoing war with Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump's Tariff Strategy Crumbles Before High-Stakes Xi Summit
Finance & Economy.detail: Legal defeats at home leave the White House with dwindling leverage as trade talks begin in Beijing with President Xi Jinping to discuss trade, the war in Iran and, possibly, Taiwan.

Finance & Economy.headline: Canada to invest $2 million in economic hub backed by Philippines, U.S. and Japan.
Finance & Economy.detail: Canada is set to invest $2 million in an economic hub that is backed by the Philippines, the U.S., and Japan.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Core Inflation Rises to 7-Month High
Finance & Economy.detail: The annual core inflation rate in the United States, excluding food and energy, rose to 2.8% in April 2026, the sharpest increase since September of last year, with prices rising sharply for services less energy services.

Technology.headline: iOS end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging begins rolling today in beta
Technology.detail: Apple's iOS is starting to roll out end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging in beta, indicated by a new lock icon in Android chats.

Technology.headline: Dua Lipa is suing Samsung for $15 million
Technology.detail: The singer Dua Lipa is accusing Samsung of illegally using her likeness to sell TVs.

Technology.headline: Daybreak is OpenAI's response to Anthropic's Claude Mythos
Technology.detail: OpenAI has launched a cybersecurity initiative called Daybreak, which is powered by GPT-5.5 and Codex Security.

Technology.headline: Forza Horizon 6 has leaked a week ahead of launch
Technology.detail: Unencrypted files for Forza Horizon 6 were downloaded from Steam and shared by pirates a week before its official launch.

Technology.headline: Google Spots Hackers Using AI To Find Zero-Day Flaw For Mass Exploitation
Technology.detail: Google reports that hackers are using AI to find zero-day flaws for mass exploitation.

Science.headline: Scientists Reversed Biological Age in Older Adults with a 4-week Diet Change
Science.detail: A new University of Sydney study found that a four-week diet change was enough to make some older adults appear biologically younger.

Science.headline: Scientists Say This Algae Could Remove Microplastics from Drinking Water
Science.detail: Researchers created a special kind of algae that produces limonene, an orange-scented oil, that can grab microscopic plastic pollution out of water almost like a magnet.

Science.headline: Stunning Fossil Discovery Challenges the Origins of Animal Life
Science.detail: Scientists revisiting mysterious 540-million-year-old microfossils from Brazil have overturned a major idea about early animal life, determining that what were once thought to be trails left behind by tiny worm-like creatures are now believed to be fossilized.

Science.headline: NASA's Hubble Reveals a Giant Chaotic Planet Nursery Unlike Anything Seen Before
Science.detail: Hubble has revealed a giant planet-forming disk unlike anything astronomers have seen before, nicknamed “Dracula's Chivito,” the enormous structure appears turbulent and oddly lopsided.

Science.headline: Scientists Just Rewrote Biology: “Hidden” Mechanism Could Transform Diabetes Treatment
Science.detail: Researchers have uncovered an unexpected biological process that may allow the body to directly control diabetes.

Health.headline: This 800-year-old Chinese Exercise Helps Lower Blood Pressure Naturally
Health.detail: An ancient Chinese exercise routine may be just as powerful as a daily brisk walk for lowering blood pressure, without the need for equipment, gyms, or intense workouts. A clinical trial showed that adults with stage 1 hypertension who practiced baduanjin experienced similar benefits to those who walked briskly.

Health.headline: Scientists Say 8,500 Steps a Day Could Stop Weight from Creeping Back
Health.detail: A new international analysis suggests that walking about 8,500 steps a day may be a simple secret to keeping weight off after dieting. Researchers found that people who boosted their daily steps to around that level during a weight loss program maintained their weight loss more effectively.

Health.headline: FDA approves first AI test to guide breast cancer chemotherapy
Health.detail: The FDA has approved the first artificial intelligence test designed to guide breast cancer chemotherapy, potentially improving treatment decisions. This innovative tool could help personalize cancer treatment plans based on individual patient needs.

Health.headline: The magic of mushrooms: Psychedelic psilocybin shows promise for treating cocaine addiction
Health.detail: Recent studies suggest that psilocybin, a psychedelic compound found in mushrooms, may hold promise in treating cocaine addiction. Researchers are exploring its potential to reshape brain activity and support mental health in individuals struggling with addiction.

Health.headline: WHO chief says "work not over" after evacuation of hantavirus-stricken ship
Health.detail: The head of the World Health Organization states that more work is needed to contain hantavirus after evacuations took place from a cruise ship that was affected by a deadly outbreak. The WHO is continuing efforts to monitor and prevent the spread of the virus.

Sports.headline: Champion Thunder hold off tenacious Lakers 115-110 in Game 4 for another playoff series sweep
Sports.detail: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 35 points as the Oklahoma City Thunder swept the Los Angeles Lakers out of the second round of the NBA playoffs with a 115-110 victory in Game 4 on Monday night; LeBron James had 24 points and 14 rebounds in the final game of his 23rd season.

Sports.headline: Two embattled Sydney clubs want a disgruntled Warriors star … but there's a big $1.6m 'problem'
Sports.detail: The Bulldogs and Dragons are reportedly interested in Luke Metcalf, who has been given permission by the Warriors to test the market following the club's re-signing of Tanah Boyd. Metcalf's relationship with the Warriors appears strained, as indicated by a frosty press release and his exclusion from the squad facing Brisbane.

Sports.headline: GT vs SRH Playing 11 Updates, IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans, Sunrisers Hyderabad unlikely to tinker with winning combinations
Sports.detail: Gujarat Titans will face Sunrisers Hyderabad at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad on Tuesday. Both teams will be looking to strengthen their chances of making the playoffs.

Sports.headline: Bangladesh suffer humiliating first-ever Test defeat in Bangladesh
Sports.detail: Bangladesh secured their first-ever Test victory against Pakistan on home soil, winning by 104 runs in the opening Test at the Shere Bangla National Stadium in Dhaka. This win also marked Bangladesh's third consecutive Test victory over Pakistan.

Sports.headline: Mitchell ties NBA playoff mark with 39 points in 2nd half as Cavs even series vs. Pistons 112-103
Sports.detail: Donovan Mitchell tied an NBA playoff record with 39 second-half points as the Cavaliers defeated the Pistons 112-103 to even their series.

Entertainment.headline: UAE museums offer free visits for International Museum Day
Entertainment.detail: Guests can enter for free in these UAE museums this weekend for International Museum Day, celebrated annually on May 18.

Entertainment.headline: WEBTOON Entertainment Inc. to Participate in Upcoming Investor Conferences
Entertainment.detail: WEBTOON Entertainment Inc. will participate in the J.P. Morgan 2026 Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference on May 20, 2026, and the UBS Asian Investment Conference 2026. The presentations will be webcast live on the company's investor relations website.

Entertainment.headline: VENU Announces Expansion Plans of Its Premium Live Entertainment Portfolio into Tennessee with Landmark $300 Million Amphitheater at the Bend in Chattanooga
Entertainment.detail: VENU Holding Corporation is expanding into Tennessee with a $300 million amphitheater project named Sunset Amphitheater Chattanooga, featuring approximately 12,500 seats and a canopied roof for year-round events. The project is in partnership with Urban Story Ventures and is contingent upon successful completion of public-private partnership incentives.

Entertainment.headline: List of DVD release dates for May 12 and beyond
Entertainment.detail: ArcaMax Publishing provides a list of DVD release dates for May 12 and beyond, including titles such as 19: The Bride! and 19: Fallout: Complete Second Season.

Entertainment.headline: Matt Damon and Ben Affleck sued over movie
Entertainment.detail: Real-life officers are suing Ben Affleck and Matt Damon through their production company over Netflix's "The Rip," which is inspired by a true story and follows two Miami-Dade police officers who discover cartel cash and corruption.

Crime & Justice.headline: Bomb rigged to rickshaw explodes in northwest Pakistan bazaar, killing at least 9 people
Crime & Justice.detail: A bomb hidden within a motorized rickshaw detonated in a bustling bazaar in northwest Pakistan, resulting in the deaths of at least nine individuals and injuring numerous others.

Crime & Justice.headline: Philippine senator vows to fight International Criminal Court order to arrest him over killings
Crime & Justice.detail: A Philippine senator, wanted by the International Criminal Court for alleged crimes against humanity, has vowed to resist any attempts to bring him before the tribunal, asserting he never endorsed extrajudicial killings during his time as the country's police chief.

Crime & Justice.headline: Soil testing at California house turns up evidence of human remains in Kristin Smart case
Crime & Justice.detail: Evidence of human remains has been discovered during soil testing at a California residence in connection with the Kristin Smart case.

Crime & Justice.headline: 'Call up from All Blacks': Man makes rugby tackle on fleeing suspect running from police.
Crime & Justice.detail: A man in New Zealand made a rugby tackle on a suspect fleeing from police.

Crime & Justice.headline: Labour slams Tories' 'abysmal' crime record as just 5% of cases solved.
Crime & Justice.detail: Labour party criticizes the Conservative party's crime record, citing that only 5% of cases are solved.

Education.headline: NEET-UG 2026 under scanner as Rajasthan SOG probes 'guess paper' leak
Education.detail: The Rajasthan Special Operations Group (SOG) is investigating a potential leak of a 'guess paper' related to the NEET-UG 2026 exam.

Education.headline: Number of students in higher education more than doubled in 20 years
Education.detail: UNESCO's Higher Education Global Trends Report indicates that the number of students enrolled in higher education has more than doubled in the last 20 years, reaching 269 million in 2024, while international student mobility has tripled. The report also highlights that women outnumber men in higher education but are still underrepresented at the doctoral level.

Education.headline: New law will require Virginia colleges to ask students if they're parents
Education.detail: A new law in Virginia will require public colleges and universities to ask students if they are parents, allowing colleges to better understand and serve the needs of parenting students by informing resource and program development. The goal is to make parenting students, who are often invisible on college campuses, more visible and successful.

Education.headline: CBSE Class 12 Result Delay Triggers Meme Fest On X: "GTA 6 Will Come Before This"
Education.detail: The delay in the CBSE Class 12 results has led to a surge of memes on X (formerly Twitter), with students humorously comparing the situation to the long-awaited video game Grand Theft Auto 6.

Education.headline: Wakatobi Marks National Education Day with Handover of Ocean Conservation Learning Materials
Education.detail: UNESCO handed over ocean conservation learning materials to the Government of Wakatobi in Indonesia on National Education Day, as part of the Sustaining Our Oceans (SOO) initiative, to integrate marine ecosystem education into the junior secondary school curriculum. The materials will be distributed to all junior secondary schools across the Wakatobi Islands.

Lifestyle.headline: Raymond Lifestyle shares down 30% in six months: Time to buy, sell or hold? Roadmap for investors
Lifestyle.detail: Shares of Raymond Lifestyle Ltd were up in Tuesday's trade but remain down nearly 30% over the last six months; analysts suggest accumulating on dips for the long term, awaiting a stronger trend reversal above Rs 820-840. The company's Q4 FY26 revenue growth exceeded expectations, though operating performance fell short.

Lifestyle.headline: Tom Holland's BERO Is Using Events to Build a Lifestyle Brand
Lifestyle.detail: Tom Holland's nonalcoholic beer brand BERO hosted its second annual Padel Classic, blending celebrity competition with hospitality to build a lifestyle experience rooted in sports and social connection. The event debuted BERO's premium glass bottle, signaling a move into upscale hospitality and reflecting the increasing integration of nonalcoholic beverages into mainstream social settings.

Lifestyle.headline: CNN launches CNN Weather app
Lifestyle.detail: CNN has launched its standalone weather app in the U.S., marking its first such product since overhauling its digital strategy last year. This launch is crucial in CNN's transition to becoming a digital-first news organization, depending on its ability to build audiences across lifestyle-focused products.

Lifestyle.headline: 'It's so full of love', the festival for young carers
Lifestyle.detail: The Young Carers Festival in Hampshire, UK, offers a residential weekend for young carers aged 11-17 to escape their responsibilities and experience the joy of being a child. The festival provides a supportive environment with activities like live music, fun fair rides, and creative workshops, but relies on grants and local groups to help attendees due to budget constraints and high travel costs.

Lifestyle.headline: Off-road market shifts from toughness to lifestyle
Lifestyle.detail: The off-road vehicle market is evolving to integrate lifestyle elements, focusing on enhancing the overall experience without sacrificing core attributes like reliability and capability. While technological advancements are being introduced, the shift prioritizes practical improvements for convenience and comfort, acknowledging that reliability remains a key factor for South African buyers.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Silverman: It's hard to say goodbye to Denver icons, but memories will endure
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The demolition of Elway's Cherry Creek and the death of a close friend bring back memories.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: We Americans must check our hate
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The author argues that Americans are capable of hate and should cleanse their hearts of it, especially the hate to which we feel most self-righteously justified.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Trying to persuade voters by mail? Beware.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The author admits they were wrong about political ads not changing anyone's mind, as they recently received mailers that made them want to learn more about the candidates for governor.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Letters to the Editor: Obama is politicking too much? Look at Trump after his first term
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Several letters respond to an op-ed criticizing Obama's continued political engagement, with writers defending his right to speak out and contrasting his actions with those of former President Trump.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Guest Editorial: Good-bye, Pittsburgh
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Post-Gazette Editorial Board bids farewell, thanking readers for their support, appreciation, and outrage, and for holding them accountable.

World.headline: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'life support'
World.detail: President Trump warned that the ceasefire with Iran is on "life support" after rejecting Iran's latest peace proposal, citing concerns over Iran's nuclear program and demands regarding the Strait of Hormuz. The US President is expected to discuss the Iran crisis with Chinese President Xi Jinping during an upcoming visit to Beijing.

World.headline: World Bank warns Pacific growth to slow to 2.8 pct in 2026
World.detail: The World Bank projects that economic growth in the Pacific region will slow to 2.8 percent in 2026 due to rising fuel and shipping costs, supply chain disruptions and global uncertainty. The report notes that Pacific nations are especially vulnerable to fuel price volatility, with oil imports accounting for a significant portion of merchandise imports.

World.headline: Passengers disembark cruise ship at center of Hantavirus outbreak
World.detail: Passengers who were quarantined on the MV Hondius cruise ship due to a Hantavirus outbreak have finally begun returning to their home countries after the ship docked in Spain's Canary Islands. Three people have died, and several others have been infected since the ship left Argentina last month, though officials say that the risk to the general public remains very low.

World.headline: Pakistan scrambles to salvage US-Iran diplomacy as ceasefire faces collapse
World.detail: Pakistan has denied allegations that it sheltered Iranian military aircraft from potential US strikes, as the ceasefire it helped broker between the US and Iran appears increasingly at risk. This follows President Trump dismissing Iran's latest peace proposal and a report that Iran had moved military aircraft to a Pakistani airbase.

World.headline: China Warns Japan Over Arms Export Move
World.detail: China has criticized Japan's decision to ease its long-standing ban on exporting weapons. Tensions in the region remain high amid ongoing geopolitical shifts.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Takes Precautions After Three Health Workers Linked To Hantavirus Case Return Home
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe's Ministry of Health and Child Care (MoHCC) says three Zimbabwean healthcare workers who recently returned from Ascension Island are now under quarantine and active monitoring after being identified as contacts of a confirmed hantavirus case while abroad, and they are being monitored as part of international public health protocols. The three individuals are currently healthy but will remain under observation for 45 days while infection prevention procedures are implemented.

Zimbabwe.headline: Capitalk FM Presenter Ordered to Reject Wicknell Chivayo Car or Resign as Zimpapers Demands Return of Cash Gift
Zimbabwe.detail: A Capitalk FM presenter has been ordered to reject a car gift from Wicknell Chivayo or resign, as Zimpapers demands the return of the cash gift. This comes after Chivayo gifted Dynamos Football Club a new bus and US$50,000.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zinara disburses over ZiG2bn for road maintenance
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe National Road Administration (ZINARA) has disbursed over ZiG2 billion for road maintenance.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police launch manhunt for suspected drug lords
Zimbabwe.detail: Police have launched a manhunt for suspected drug lords.

Zimbabwe.headline: President Mnangagwa confirms 4,48 tons of gold reserves in latest RBZ visit
Zimbabwe.detail: President Emmerson Mnangagwa says Zimbabwe's gold reserves held at the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) have risen to 4.48 tonnes.
